previously_flagged_now_resolved: 04_species_concepts: 'bacteria are asexual' misread is fixed — both content_hint and narration now say 'prokaryotes (which exchange genes by horizontal transfer, not Mayr-style interbreeding), and fossils.' The recap mirrors this. Closed.

09_pace: saltation / overnight macromutation trap is now explicitly disarmed — narration says 'punctuated equilibrium is NOT saltation, and it is NOT an overnight macro-mutation. Even inside that rapid burst, change is still population-level gradual evolution — it only looks fast because we are comparing it against geological time scales.' Recap echoes 'burst is still gradual, just compressed.' Closed.

10_phylogeny_basics: 'tips are more evolved' AND 'humans descended from chimps' traps are both inoculated — content_hint now lists 'every living tip is at the present — none is ancestral to another; we did NOT evolve FROM chimpanzees, we share a most recent common ancestor with them' and 'Time runs ALONG branches from root to tip, not across the row of tips.' Narration calls out all three traps (rotation, no-tip-is-more-evolved, chimps-are-cousins-not-ancestors) explicitly. Closed.

11_clades_compare: synapomorphy/plesiomorphy relativity is now explicit — both content_hint and narration use the vertebral-column-as-synapomorphy-for-Vertebrata-but-plesiomorphy-for-Mammalia framing, with the narration spelling out 'Same trait, different clade — different label.' ap_traps_covered entry mirrors it. Closed.

15_origin_of_life: Miller-Urey early-Earth-atmosphere caveat is in — 'modern geochemistry suggests early Earth's atmosphere was probably less reducing than Miller and Urey assumed, more dominated by CO2 and nitrogen. But the experiment still stands as the first demonstration that organic monomers can form abiotically under plausible early-Earth conditions.' The principle-vs-specific-mixture framing is exactly right. Closed.

08_sympatric: autopolyploidy vs allopolyploidy distinction is now in — both content_hint and narration distinguish 'autopolyploidy (doubling within one species)' from 'allopolyploidy (hybridization between species followed by chromosome doubling),' with bread wheat correctly labeled allopolyploid/allohexaploid. Vocabulary list updated. Closed.
overall_assessment: All six pedagogical inoculations from Round 1 are landed cleanly. The saltation clarification in 09_pace is especially strong — it disarms the misread without undermining the punctuated-equilibrium concept itself. The synapomorphy-relativity move in 11_clades_compare is the most pedagogically important add of the round and the narration nails the 'same trait, different clade, different label' framing. The phylogeny tip-traps in 10 are now triple-armored (rotation + no-tip-is-more-evolved + chimps-are-cousins) and the chimp clarification in particular is the kind of thing a sharp student would respect — it shows the lesson knows what misconceptions exist in the wild. The Miller-Urey caveat is honest without being destabilizing. ap_traps_covered, key_vocabulary, and 16_recap all reflect the changes, which means a student reviewing only the recap card still gets the inoculations. Nothing new for me to flag — pedagogy is sound. Pass.
